name: modern-white-villa
desc: Modern white minimalist villa, two stories, large glass windows, flat roof with subtle overhang, symmetrical facade, manicured front lawn, infinity pool visible at ground level, Mediterranean climate landscaping
prompt:
Front-facing architectural portrait of a modern white minimalist two-story villa — perfectly symmetrical facade with flat roof and subtle overhang, large floor-to-ceiling glass windows, manicured Mediterranean front lawn, low Mediterranean planting, infinity pool at ground level in the foreground reflecting the facade. Golden hour daylight with warm sunlight illuminating the front of the building.

Camera & lens: Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt-shift lens (tilt-shift correction applied for zero perspective distortion — critical for straight verticals).

Camera settings: f/8–f/11 for maximum sharpness, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release. Use tilt/shift to correct perspective so all verticals are perfectly parallel.

Camera position (critical): camera placed dead-center on the building’s center axis, positioned directly in front and looking straight at the facade, perfectly level (no Dutch angle), eye level or slightly below (1.2–1.5 m height). Symmetrical, mirror-balanced framing with the entrance/front door as the focal point.

Lighting: golden-hour warm light striking the front facade (sun positioned to illuminate the facade, not backlit), warm tones on white stucco and glass, soft fill from the sky to minimize harsh shadows and preserve detail.

Composition: strict symmetry, building perfectly centered, foreground infinity pool and manicured lawn/path create leading lines toward the centered entrance, pool surface reflecting the facade, clear sky or subtle clouds for a clean luxury listing hero image. Ultra-high detail, natural warm color grading, pristine minimalist aesthetic, full architectural sharpness across the entire facade.

name: beach-house-timber
desc: Elevated contemporary beach house on stilts, weathered timber cladding, wrap-around porch, large sliding glass doors open to reveal interior, sand dunes and ocean behind, coastal grasses in foreground
prompt:
Elevated contemporary beach house on stilts, weathered timber cladding, wrap-around porch, large sliding glass doors fully open to reveal a glimpse of the interior, sand dunes and ocean directly behind, coastal grasses in the foreground — hero image, luxury property listing.

Camera & lens: Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt-shift lens (tilt‑shift correction used to keep verticals perfectly straight).

Camera settings: f/8, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release, shutter speed as required for golden-hour exposure; shoot RAW for maximum latitude.

Camera position (critical): camera placed exactly on the center axis of the building, dead-center, perfectly level, no Dutch angle; height eye level or slightly below (1.2–1.5 m). Apply tilt-shift correction for zero perspective distortion so all vertical lines are perfectly parallel. Symmetrical, mirror-balanced framing with the facade filling the frame horizontally.

Lighting: golden-hour warm daylight illuminating the FRONT of the facade (not backlit), warm tones on timber, soft fill from the sky to reduce harsh shadows, gentle directional side warmth to enhance texture.

Composition: building perfectly centered; symmetrical balance; foreground coastal grasses and a sweep of sand or path leading the eye to the entrance; wrap-around porch and stilts visible; sliding glass doors open and composed as the focal point/entry; clear sky or subtle clouds to keep attention on the facade; emphasize texture of weathered timber, warm highlights, and natural reflections in glass.

Styling/mood: elegant, tranquil, premium coastal living — crisp, ultra-sharp architectural portrait with corrected verticals and even exposure suitable for a luxury listing hero shot.

name: luxury-mobile-home
desc: Ultra-luxury modern mobile home / prefab, sleek rectangular form, dark metal and wood paneling, floor-to-ceiling glass front wall, elevated on a platform deck, desert mesa landscape, Joshua trees
prompt:
Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift — tripod-mounted, cable release. f/8, ISO 100, shutter speed to match golden‑hour exposure. Camera positioned dead center on the building’s center axis, perfectly level (no Dutch angle), eye level / slightly below (1.2–1.5 m). Use tilt‑shift correction so verticals are perfectly straight with zero perspective distortion; symmetrical, mirror‑balanced framing.

Scene: ultra‑luxury modern mobile prefab — sleek rectangular form elevated on a platform deck, dark matte metal and warm wood paneling, full floor‑to‑ceiling glass front wall revealing interior depth. Desert mesa landscape with scattered Joshua trees placed to frame the composition symmetrically. Foreground: low platform deck and wide stepped walkway leading directly to the entrance — clear visual lead‑in to the front door, which is the focal point. Keep the building centered, filling the frame horizontally and vertically with balanced margins.

Lighting: golden hour daylight with warm sunlight illuminating the FRONT facade (not backlit), soft warm highlights on metal and wood, gentle reflections in glass, soft sky fill to minimize harsh shadows. Slight clouds allowed to add texture but maintain a clean, luxurious look.

Composition notes: strict frontal, head‑on architectural portrait — no 3/4 angles, no perspective skew. Symmetrical placement of Joshua trees and desert elements to reinforce balance. High dynamic range retained but natural — crisp, ultra‑sharp details across facade, materials and textures. Output should read like a hero image for a luxury property listing: pristine, serene, aspirational, editorial quality.

name: glass-skyscraper-penthouse
desc: Modern glass residential skyscraper, 40+ floors, shot from ground level looking up at the facade, reflective curtain wall glass, balconies with greenery, evening sky reflected in the glass
prompt:
Camera & lens:
- Camera: Canon EOS R5
- Lens: Canon TS-E 17mm f/4L tilt-shift (tilt-shift correction applied to achieve zero perspective distortion)

Camera settings and support:
- Aperture f/11 for maximum sharpness across the facade
- ISO 100 for the cleanest image
- Tripod-mounted, cable release, mirror lock/IBIS off
- High-resolution capture, raw

Camera position — critical (HEAD-ON, perfectly centered):
- Camera positioned exactly on the building’s center axis, dead-on front view — NOT a 3/4 angle
- Camera height low eye-level, approx. 1.2–1.5 m, slightly below midpoint for a grand upward perspective while keeping symmetry
- Camera perfectly level (no Dutch tilt), lens shift used to correct converging verticals so all lines are true verticals
- Framing perfectly symmetrical and mirror-balanced in the frame

Lighting:
- Golden-hour evening light illuminating the FRONT of the building (warm, low-angle sunlight hitting the facade)
- Evening sky reflected in the curtain-wall glass — warm tones mixed with cool-blue reflections
- Soft, even fill from the open sky to avoid harsh contrasts and preserve detail in glass reflections and balcony greenery

Composition and scene details:
- Modern glass residential skyscraper, 40+ floors, reflective curtain-wall glass with balconies featuring planted greenery
- Foreground: plaza/path/paved approach or shallow reflecting pool leading directly to the main entrance, creating a leading line to the door
- Entrance/front door aligned with center axis and acting as focal point; composition emphasizes verticality and rhythm of floors and balconies
- Symmetrical repetition of glazing and balconies, clean uncluttered foreground, minimal pedestrians, luxury-property hero image aesthetic
- Clear sky with subtle evening clouds as background to enhance reflections and mood, high dynamic range to retain facade detail and reflective highlights

name: tuscan-stone-farmhouse
desc: Restored Tuscan stone farmhouse, honey-colored facade, green wooden shutters, terracotta roof, arched doorway in the center, potted olive trees flanking the entrance, gravel courtyard in foreground, cypress trees behind
prompt: Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II (tilt‑shift) — front-facing exterior architectural portrait. Camera mounted on tripod, cable release; ISO 100, f/11 for maximum sharpness, shutter speed as required for correct exposure. Camera positioned directly on the center axis of the farmhouse, perfectly level (no Dutch angle), eye level/slightly below at 1.2–1.5 m height. Apply tilt‑shift correction to eliminate perspective distortion so all verticals are perfectly straight. Compose dead center: symmetrical, mirror-balanced framing with the entire honey‑colored Tuscan stone facade filling the frame. Arched doorway centered and framed as the focal point, green wooden shutters evenly spaced, terracotta roof visible across top. Foreground: gravel courtyard leading toward the entrance as a strong leading element; potted olive trees flanking the arched doorway exactly left and right. Background: tall cypress trees rising behind the house, clear sky with subtle clouds. Lighting: warm golden hour daylight striking the FRONT of the facade (not backlit), soft even warm tones on stone and shutters, gentle sky fill to avoid harsh shadows. Produce a luxurious hero image suitable for a premium property listing: pristine, richly textured stone, crisp shadows and highlights, perfect symmetry, ultra‑sharp detail across the facade.

name: alpine-chalet-front
desc: Traditional alpine chalet with modern renovation, dark stained timber, stone base, large triangular balcony with glass railing, snow-capped mountain peaks behind, wildflower meadow in foreground
prompt:
Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift lens. f/11, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release. Tilt‑shift correction applied to achieve perfectly straight verticals.

Camera positioned dead center on the building’s center axis, perfectly level (no Dutch angle), height 1.4 m (eye level / slightly below). Frame the facade head‑on — mirror‑balanced, symmetrical composition with the entrance centered. Use shift to include the full chalet height and the snow‑capped peaks above while keeping verticals parallel.

Golden‑hour daylight with the sun illuminating the front facade (no backlighting). Warm, soft golden tones on dark stained timber and the stone base; soft sky fill to avoid harsh shadows and preserve detail in timber grain and stone texture. Use a subtle reflector or low‑power fill if needed to lift shadow under the large triangular balcony and glass railing.

Compose so a wildflower meadow in the foreground leads toward the central entrance — foreground path or natural leading line centered and guiding the eye. The large triangular balcony with glass railing must be prominent and centered above the entrance. Capture full facade width, the stone base, timber cladding details, window symmetry, and the mountain peaks rising behind the roofline against a clear sky with subtle clouds.

Technical notes: highest resolution capture for luxury listing (maximize detail), focus stacked or single-shot at f/11 to render foreground meadow and distant peaks sharp. Ensure absolute symmetry in post crop — door/central axis exactly in the middle, equal negative space left/right. Produce a hero image: clean, elevated, calm, luxurious alpine character.

name: tropical-villa-pool
desc: Balinese-modern tropical villa, open-air pavilion design, thatched roof meets clean concrete, rectangular reflecting pool perfectly centered in front of the entrance, frangipani trees, lush tropical garden
prompt:
Canon EOS R5 with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt-shift lens. Tripod-mounted, cable release.

Settings: f/11, ISO 100, shutter speed as required for golden-hour exposure (meter for warm highlights and shadow detail). Mirror-balanced RAW capture, highest resolution.

Camera position (CRITICAL): Positioned dead-center on the building’s center axis, camera height 1.3 m (eye level to slightly below), perfectly level—no Dutch angle. Tilt-shift correction applied to remove perspective distortion so all verticals are perfectly straight. Framing exactly symmetrical, facade filling frame with equal margins left/right.

Scene & composition: Front-facing, head-on portrait of a Balinese-modern tropical villa — open-air pavilion design where a traditional thatched alang-alang roof meets smooth, clean concrete volumes. A long rectangular reflecting pool is perfectly centered in the foreground, aligned with the entrance and leading the eye to the front door. Frangipani trees and lush tropical garden flank both sides, balanced symmetrically. Entrance / doorway is the focal point, centered in the frame. Foreground leading lines from pool and stone path emphasize approach. Clear sky with subtle high clouds behind, no distracting background elements.

Lighting: Golden-hour warm daylight illuminating the front facade (sun positioned to light the facade head-on, not backlit). Warm tones on thatch and concrete, soft sky fill to retain detail in shadows, minimal harsh shadows, gentle specular highlights on water in pool.

Mood & output: Hero image for a luxury property listing — crisp, ultra-sharp, natural colors, high dynamic range, calm luxe aesthetic, perfect symmetry and architectural precision.

name: brownstone-townhouse
desc: Classic New York brownstone townhouse row, ornate cornices, stoop with wrought-iron railings, bay windows, autumn trees lining the street, warm interior light glowing through windows
prompt:
Phase One XF IQ4 150MP + Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift — front‑on architectural portrait. Camera settings: f/11, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release; shutter speed set for golden‑hour exposure. Camera positioned exactly on the center axis of a Classic New York brownstone townhouse row, dead‑center, looking straight at the facade — perfectly symmetrical, no Dutch angle. Height 1.3 m (eye level / slightly below) for a natural, grand perspective. Lens tilt‑shift correction applied to eliminate perspective convergence and keep all verticals perfectly straight.

Lighting: golden‑hour warm light illuminating the FRONT of the brownstone facades (no backlighting), warm tones on brick, stone cornices and wrought‑iron railings, soft sky fill to minimize harsh shadows, gentle contrast. Warm interior lights visible, glowing through windows to create a welcoming luxury listing mood.

Composition: exact center framing so the stoop and front entrance are the focal point; symmetrical bay windows, ornate cornices, and matching wrought‑iron railings mirrored left/right in frame. Foreground: stoop steps and sidewalk forming a leading line to the entrance; autumn trees lining the street with colorful leaves framing the facade symmetrically. Clear sky with subtle clouds behind. Ultra‑sharp, high‑resolution, editorial luxury real‑estate hero image — crisp architectural detail, even exposure across facade, balanced warm evening atmosphere.

name: concrete-brutalist-house
desc: Bold brutalist concrete residence, dramatic cantilevered upper floor, board-formed concrete texture, narrow vertical windows, geometric facade, reflecting pool in front mirroring the structure, minimalist landscaping
prompt: Front-facing architectural portrait of a bold brutalist concrete residence photographed dead-center, looking straight at the facade — perfectly symmetrical, mirror-balanced composition, hero image for a luxury property listing. Phase One XF IQ4 150MP with Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift (tilt‑shift correction applied for zero perspective distortion, verticals perfectly straight). Settings: f/11 for maximum sharpness across the entire facade, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release. Camera positioned on the center axis of the building, perfectly level (no Dutch angle), eye height ~1.3 m, straight-on framing. Lighting: golden hour warm daylight illuminating the FRONT of building (not backlit), warm tones on board‑formed concrete texture, soft sky fill with no harsh shadows. Composition: dramatic cantilevered upper floor, board‑formed concrete texture emphasized, narrow vertical windows and geometric facade rendered with razor-sharp detail. Foreground reflecting pool centered and leading directly to the entrance, mirror-like reflection of the structure, minimalist landscaping (low grasses, clean paving) framing the pool but not distracting. Entrance/front door is the focal point at the visual center. Background: clear sky or subtle clouds, soft gradient of warm light. Style: formal architectural portrait, high dynamic range realism, pristine, calm, editorial luxury aesthetic — no people, no cars, minimal distractions.

name: spanish-colonial-mansion
desc: Grand Spanish colonial mansion, symmetrical white stucco facade, red clay tile roof, arched colonnade entrance with carved stone pillars, large wooden double doors, courtyard fountain visible, bougainvillea climbing walls
prompt:
Grand Spanish Colonial mansion — symmetrical white stucco facade, red clay tile roof, wide arched colonnade entrance with carved stone pillars, grand wooden double doors centered, courtyard fountain visible beyond the entrance, bougainvillea climbing the walls. Golden hour daylight, warm front illumination on the facade, soft sky fill, warm tones, no harsh shadows. Hero image for a luxury property listing: ultra-detailed, high-resolution, refined editorial look.

Camera & lens: Canon EOS R5; Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift lens (tilt‑shift is critical). Apply tilt‑shift correction for zero perspective distortion and perfectly straight verticals.

Camera settings: f/8–f/11 for maximum sharpness across the entire facade, ISO 100, tripod-mounted, cable release, mirrorless live view for precise composition.

Camera position (CRITICAL): Camera positioned dead center on the building’s center axis, directly in front, looking straight at the facade — perfectly symmetrical, mirror-balanced framing. Camera perfectly level (no Dutch angle). Eye level or slightly below (1.2–1.5 m height) for a natural but grand perspective. Tilt‑shift correction applied to eliminate converging lines.

Composition: Building perfectly centered, entrance/front doors as focal point, foreground courtyard/path leading to entrance and fountain visible, bougainvillea framing edges, clear sky or subtle clouds behind. Emphasize crisp stucco texture, carved stone detail, wood grain, tile highlights, and gentle reflections in the fountain.
